data.title: check-usage.sh reads incorrect values reporting inflated usage percentages (3100%/1400% observed)
data.description:
## Symptom
check-usage.sh reported USAGE_5HR: 3100% and USAGE_7DAY: 1400% with VERDICT: 2 (paused tier),
causing sprint sub-agent dispatch to halt with MAX_AGENTS: 0. Actual usage is not over 100%.

## Observed log
```
USAGE_SOURCE: api
USAGE_5HR: 3100%
USAGE_7DAY: 1400%
VERDICT: 2
usage_exit=2
```

## Investigation scope
Compare the values used in the percentage calculation with the actual values returned by the
Claude OAuth usage endpoint. Specifically:
- What numerator and denominator are used to compute the 5hr and 7day percentages?
- Are the throttle thresholds (90%/95% for throttled, 95%/98% for paused) applied against the
  correct field types (e.g., tokens vs. requests vs. cost)?
- Are there unit mismatches (e.g., raw token counts compared against percentage-scale thresholds)?
- Does the API response schema match what the script expects (field names, nesting, value ranges)?

## Expected behavior
Usage percentages accurately reflect actual consumption. Values should not exceed 100% under
normal operation. VERDICT should correctly reflect the throttle tier.

## Impact
Sprint and debug-everything sub-agent dispatch was incorrectly halted mid-session, blocking all
parallel execution for epic 97fc-b2ca.
